action_items.action: WELCOME BACK — Vic Dadson returns today from ~2 weeks sick leave (signed off from 21/04, fortnight ends 05/05). No Slack messages from Vic since 23/04. Plan a brief, low-pressure check-in: cover her wellbeing first, then the data engineering discussion Mike flagged (speak to Nemanja for clear reqs + knowledge sharing). Review her open backlog items before the call so she can hit the ground running without overload.

URGENT — Reply to Jessica Dordevic DM (30/04 15:05 BST): 'Let me know about the banners please so I'll check with Ciara after.' Jessica is awaiting Mike's sign-off on GDCU-8928 IUJ Banners. She confirmed updates to GDCU-8546 (Tinder) are 'should be done from what we discussed' and flagged she couldn't use the green container ('it's actually a clock'). Banners decision is the outstanding item holding her up for today.

ESCALATE NOW — GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign (Ciara Carroll) is Blocked for Day 19 (since 16/04/2026). Carried across bank holiday weekend with no movement. This is the longest-standing open blocker on the board. Chloe flagged to Mike on 29/04: 'the only thing I'm unsure about is the Op List Redesign — beholden to Michael B on that.' Identify whether Michael Butlin's input is what's blocking this, and either get that input today or escalate to Colin Brannigan to unblock.

REVIEW — GDCU-8895 Prediction Market Page Not on US Sitemap (Sanja Budic, High, In Product since ~01/05/2026). Sanja completed implementation: Prediction header on US sitemap, /us/prediction-markets linked, Prediction Reviews logic in place. Compliance note: must NOT appear under 'Betting' headers. Colin Brannigan is a stakeholder — sign off today to avoid it ageing into next week.

DECISION NEEDED — Joshua Smith posted in #gdc-product-design-pod (01/05 09:23 BST): 'We need to make a decision on the designs for GDCU-8787 Carousel Banner Shortcode.' Options: (A) reuse Simple Banner shortcode + navigation arrows — reduces editor complexity; (B) use Free Games banner style — different layout, cannot reuse existing shortcode. Joshua favours Option A (mockup created, Screenshot 3 in Slack). Mike is a stakeholder — respond in the channel or DM Joshua today.

HEADS UP — Miguel Migneco is OFF today (confirmed DM 30/04: 'hey Mike I am off next Tue'). His open work: GDCU-8813 (Ace Navigation UI Audit), GDCU-8546 (Tinder admin section — Claude Design prompts shared by Mike on 29/04, Miguel had moved to Figma). Confirm with Jessica or Chloe that nothing in Miguel's queue is blocking today's sprint progress.

SIGN OFF — GDCU-8736 Learning Hub Imagery (Ciara Carroll, Medium, In Product since 28/04 — Day 7). Ciara shared Figma prototype. Chloe noted 'some back and forth' at sprint close on 30/04. Approving this unblocks Carlos Blauth on GDCU-8733 (Apply Feedback to Learning Hub). Check with Ciara what, if any, outstanding feedback remains before signing off.

CHECK IN — GDCU-8546 Tinder Style Offer Match (Jessica Dordevic, In Progress). Jessica confirmed on 30/04 that updates are 'should be done from what we discussed' (hide pass/interested buttons post-match, remove 'betting' from label). Chloe expected this to be closed shortly. Ask Jessica this morning if she is ready to move to In Product. Note: Mike updated Chloe on 30/04 — no A/B test, one simple card with logo/offer/T&Cs, user taken to operator landing page after a match animation.

WORLD CUP PRIORITIES — Mike flagged to Chloe on 29/04: 'We need to prio anything WC related now — the FTP game, the Cosmo reskin and Group Bets.' Mike also noted he needed to create a ticket for the FTP WC game. With sprint 170 open and first day back, confirm with Chloe that these WC tickets are on the plan and in the correct priority order for the upcoming cycle.

DM GABRIEL — Intake form questions from 23/04 remain unresolved. Gabriel added two suggestions: (1) SEO considerations & Information Architecture for new pages/hubs, (2) for operator offers, which attributes should be displayed. Mike started this standardisation effort with Chloe. Confirm the intake form is updated and shared back to Gabriel today.

FOLLOW UP — GDCU-8879 Genesis Brand Carousel Component Card (Jessica Dordevic, In Progress). Carried into sprint 170 (confirmed by Chloe 30/04). Was bounced from In Product on 29/04. Chloe's outstanding feedback: confirm logo name/size sourced from Origins. Check with Jessica where this stands today given Miguel is absent.

PRODUCT SYNC PREP — Wednesday product sync likely tomorrow (06/05). Chloe typically posts in #core-product asking for agenda points the day before. First day back from bank holiday — prepare Mike's points now: GDCU-8619 blocker escalation, WC ticket prioritisation, Vic Dadson's return and data engineering reqs, sprint 170 open items. Confluence agenda link will follow from Chloe.

CREATIVE DIRECTION — GDCU-8846 Cosmo Spins Spring Ad Campaign (Ciara Carroll, To Do). Carried from previous sprint. Josh Kerr feedback: needs rethinking — clearer message, less 'gaming' feel. With Ciara managing GDCU-8619, GDCU-8736, and GDCU-8947, a direction decision from Mike is needed before she can proceed.

MONITOR — GDCU-8947 Cosmo Cash Machine Images (Ciara Carroll, In Progress). Created by Ciara on 01/05 (bank holiday Friday). Scope: update article image, hero image, fonts, coming soon images, and email images for Jackpot £5,000 draw. No action required yet but note it is active in Ciara's queue alongside three other tickets.
